preamble: The shift from the voiced labiodental fricative [v] to the labio-velar approximant [w] is a form of vocalization and lenition. This process often occurs in intervocalic positions or as a historical development stage where fricatives weaken into glides.

examples.note: Regional variation where /v/ weakens to a glide in certain dialects.
Development stages in various dialects where labiodental fricatives undergo lenition.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
